title: Ensure network access for Cloud Object Storage is  restricted to specific IP range

input_row.title: Ensure network access for Cloud Object Storage is  restricted to specific IP range
input_row.description: IBM Cloud Object Storage bucket firewall restricts all access to data unless the request originates from a list of allowed IP addresses.
input_row.rationale: Restrict access to your data stored in Cloud Object Storage buckets and enhance security controls by creating a firewall rule that will only allow data access to the list of IP addresses you specify.
input_row.audit: To check or confirm that an IBM Cloud Object Storage bucket is accessible only from a list of authorized IP addresses, you can navigate to Access Policies section available under your bucket information and check Authorized IPs list. 1. Go to IBM Cloud Console: https://cloud.ibm.com/resources 2. Under Storage click on the Cloud Object Storage instance you want to check (you will be directed to a default bucket listing page) 3. Click on the Cloud Object Storage bucket for which you want to review the bucket firewall information 4. At the bucket information screen, click on the Access policies section on the left navigation 5. Within Bucket access policies you will see the tab Authorized IPs 6. Review the list of IPs to ensure it includes the IPs you want authorized
input_row.remediation: Follow the steps outlined to add an IP to the list of Authorized IPs in bucket firewall policies. Using the console to set a firewall From the IBM Cloud console dashboard, you can restrict access to your content by setting a firewall. Set a list of authorized IP addresses 1. Start by selecting Storage to view your resource list. 2. Next, select the service instance with your bucket from within the Storage menu. This takes you to the Object Storage Console. 3. Select the bucket that you want to limit access to authorized IP addresses. 4. Select Access policies from the navigation menu. 5. Select the Authorized IPs tab. 6. Click on Add and specify a list of IP addresses in CIDR notation, for example 192.168.0.0/16, fe80:021b::0/64. Addresses can follow either IPv4 or IPv6 standards. 7. Click Add. 8. The firewall will not be enforced until the address is saved in the console. Click Save all to enforce the firewall. Note that all objects in this bucket are only accessible from those IP addresses.
input_row.impact: For any application(s) that depend upon or access data stored in Cloud Object Storage buckets to conduct its operation(s) you must ensure that the network it is using to access Cloud Object Storage buckets is also included in the COS firewall. Not doing so could possibly result in disruption of the activities.
input_row.default_value: By default Cloud Object Storage buckets can only be accessed by authenticated users.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_cos_bucket_access_control_ip_restriction

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use IBM Cloud API calls to verify whether the Cloud Object Storage (COS) bucket firewall restricts access to specific IP addresses.
- API endpoints to call include: 
  - `GET /v1/instance/{bucket_id}/data_access` to retrieve the access configurations of the specified bucket, including the list of authorized IPs.
  - The relevant method in the IBM Cloud CLI SDK would be `ibm_cos.get_bucket_access_policy(bucket_id)` to fetch the current access policy for the bucket to determine if the Authorized IPs list matches expectations.
  
- Specific resource to enumerate: 
  - Cloud Object Storage buckets within the relevant IBM Cloud account and region.
  
- Exact configuration properties/settings to validate: 
  - Check the `authorized_ips` field in the bucket access policies; it should contain an expected set of IP addresses.
  
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values: 
  - Pass: The list of authorized IPs contains only the specified IPs in CIDR notation that are deemed allowed for access to the bucket. 
  - Fail: The list is empty or contains any unauthorized IP addresses which might breach the security control.

This comprehensive approach allows for automated verification of the access control settings for IBM Cloud Object Storage, ensuring compliance with the CIS control regarding restricted access based on specified IP addresses.
